---
title: "Introduction to Robot Learning"
subtitle: "Instructor: Yonghyeon Lee"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
---

## Course Logistics

- **Website**: [https://intro-to-robotics.github.io/mrl/](https://intro-to-robotics.github.io/mrl/)
- Speak up and participate! Please interrupt me at anytime.
- Remember, if you do not understand something, there is a 90% chance it is my fault for not being clear, so please ask questions.

## Course Organizations

- All the details will be on the Website
  - Syllabus and schedule
  - Notes and slide links
  - Announcements

- Assessment:
  - 60\% Four assignments (15\% each)
  - 20\% 5-minute video presentation
  - 20\% Project final report

## Course structure
Lecture Schedule: [https://intro-to-robotics.github.io/mrl/](https://intro-to-robotics.github.io/mrl/)

- 

## Course Staff

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Key Points

- Movement representations
- Generative policy learning
- Reinforcement learning for control
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Visual

![](https://quarto.org/quarto.png){width=65% fig-alt="Example image"}
:::
:::

## Image-Focused Template

Use this when one figure is the main message.

![](https://quarto.org/quarto.png){width=55% fig-alt="Example figure"}

Caption: Add one sentence that explains the takeaway.

## Local Image Template

Put files under `slides/images/` and reference them like this:

```markdown
![](images/lecture-01/overview.png){fig-alt="System overview" width=85%}
```

## Background Image Template {background-image="https://quarto.org/quarto.png" background-size="contain" background-opacity="0.15"}

### One-line Message

Robot learning combines structure, data, and optimization.

## Code Template

```python
import numpy as np

def score(x):
    return -x / (np.linalg.norm(x) + 1e-6)
```

- Keep code short.
- Explain one key idea per slide.

## Callout Template

::: {.callout-note}
### Practical Tip
Use one concept slide and one visual slide as a pair.
:::

## Thank You

- Questions?
- Contact: `your-email@example.com`
